methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Naïfo Technologies is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Gaëtan Sévérac that has raised €54.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of nine same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers, comprising eight private companies and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). The peer set includes later-stage reference points such as Exotec ($2.0B, Series D) and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), alongside earlier-stage data from 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a central implied valuation of $1.6B. The nine-company peer cohort exhibits a median valuation of $1.6B with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B, reflecting heterogeneity typical of early-stage robotics capital structures and growth trajectories. This single methodological lens, while constrained by data availability, anchors the analysis given alignment on sector and stage criteria.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single valuation method. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—particularly the $10.0B outlier at 1X Technologies versus the $300M floor—and limited visibility into Naïfo's relative competitive positioning, growth metrics, and unit economics relative to named comparables.
